name: Julius Hemphill
abstract: Julius Arthur Hemphill (January 24, 1938 – April 2, 1995) was a jazz composer and saxophone player. He performed mainly on alto saxophone, less often on soprano and tenor saxophones and flute.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Moses was the first who said it. He said – I don’t know who said it, but he said, “There’s organizations, the Chicago AACM,” and there was something in St. Louis, that Oliver Lake thing, right? And Julius [Hemphill].
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I like him. He’s a fine young alto player. ... I like them for their honesty and that they do what they do. ... Yeah man, I love that album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Julius was a genius but anyway we'll talk about that later, too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
